title: Simpler — Internal LFO
summary: Simpler's built-in LFO modulates pitch, filter, or amplitude over time. Used in drum racks across all 5 source tracks to add subtle organic movement to otherwise static samples.
steps: Open a Simpler (inside a Drum Rack pad, or standalone)
Click the LFO tab at the bottom of the Simpler interface
Set Destination: Pitch (subtle wobble), Filter (tonal movement), Amp (tremolo — volume oscillation)
Set Rate: how fast the LFO oscillates. Low rate (0.1–1Hz) = slow, organic movement. High rate (5Hz+) = vibrato or tremolo effect
Set Amount: the depth of modulation. For subtle movement on a hat: Pitch destination, Amount 3–5%. For a tape-stop effect on a clap: Pitch destination, Amount 20%, Rate 2Hz
Retrigger: enable to reset the LFO phase on each new note. Enable for consistent results on every hit. Disable for an evolving, never-repeating effect
tips: Pitch LFO at very low Amount (2–3%) on a closed hi-hat makes it feel like a real drummer — slightly unpredictable and organic
Filter LFO creates a sweeping tonal movement. Use on a long clap or snare reverb tail to make it feel like it's breathing
Retrigger ON = the LFO always starts from the same point on each hit. Retrigger OFF = the LFO is continuous and each hit catches it at a random phase
The LFO here is inside Simpler — it modulates the sample's playback, not an external effect. It's very CPU-efficient
practice_hint: In any rep with a drum rack (rep-01 onwards), click a hat pad, open the Simpler inside, go to the LFO tab, set Destination to Pitch, Amount to 3%. Loop 4 bars. Can you hear the difference vs no LFO?
ableton_version_note: Available in Simpler in Ableton Live Standard and Suite.
